title: Veerabhadra Temple, Pattiseema
tagline: Veerabhadra Temple, Pattiseema — spiritual town of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Veerabhadra Temple is located on Devakuta Parvatham, a hillock on an islet amidst the Godavari River near Pattiseema in Eluru district, Andhra Pradesh.
overview.description: Veerabhadra Temple is located on Devakuta Parvatham, a hillock on an islet amidst the Godavari River near Pattiseema in Eluru district, Andhra Pradesh. The temple is dedicated to Shiva and the namesake is Veerabhadra. The temple is one of the 'pancha Kasi kshetras' (five important peaceful places) mentioned in the Skaanda puranam, a collection of Hindu religious texts.

topPlaces.name: Pattiseema
topPlaces.description: Pattiseema is a village in Polavaram mandal of Eluru district, Andhra Pradesh, India. It is located on the bank of the River Godavari.

topPlaces.name: Pattiseema Lift Irrigation Project
topPlaces.description: Pattiseema Lift Irrigation Project is a river interlinking project which connects Godavari River to Krishna River. This project has thereby become the first of such irrigation type projects in the country to be completed in time without any budget enhancements.

topPlaces.name: Polavaram mandal
topPlaces.description: Polavaram mandal is one of the 27 mandals in Eluru district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is under the administration of Jangareddigudem revenue division and the headquarters are located at Polavaram.

topPlaces.name: Seethanagaram mandal
topPlaces.description: Seethanagaram is one of the 19 mandals in East Godavari district of the state of Andhra Pradesh, India. Its headquarters are located at Seethanagaram.

topPlaces.name: Polavaram Project
topPlaces.description: The Polavaram Project is an under-construction multi-purpose irrigation project on the Godavari River in the Eluru District and Polavaram District in Andhra Pradesh, India. The project has been accorded National Project status by the Central Government of India.

items.note: Pattiseema is a village in Polavaram mandal of Eluru district, Andhra Pradesh, India. It is located on the bank of the River Godavari.

Pattiseema Lift Irrigation Project is a river interlinking project which connects Godavari River to Krishna River. This project has thereby become the first of such irrigation type projects in the country to be completed in time without any budget enhancements.

Polavaram mandal is one of the 27 mandals in Eluru district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is under the administration of Jangareddigudem revenue division and the headquarters are located at Polavaram.
Seethanagaram is one of the 19 mandals in East Godavari district of the state of Andhra Pradesh, India. Its headquarters are located at Seethanagaram.

The Polavaram Project is an under-construction multi-purpose irrigation project on the Godavari River in the Eluru District and Polavaram District in Andhra Pradesh, India. The project has been accorded National Project status by the Central Government of India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Veerabhadra Temple, Pattiseema?
faq.a: The best time to visit Veerabhadra Temple, Pattiseema is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Veerabhadra Temple, Pattiseema?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajahmundry Airport (22 km) and the nearest railway station is Vijayawada Junction (134 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Veerabhadra Temple, Pattiseema?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Veerabhadra Temple, Pattiseema at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Veerabhadra Temple, Pattiseema?
faq.a: Stays in Veerabhadra Temple, Pattiseema start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Veerabhadra Temple, Pattiseema?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pattiseema, Pattiseema Lift Irrigation Project, Polavaram mandal, Seethanagaram mandal, Polavaram Project.
